free_text: Proton-induced reactions on molybdenum
Experiment performed at the University
of Notre Dame's FN tandem (10 MV) accelerator.
The thickness of all Mo targets were in the range
of 0.5 - 8.5 mg/cm2. Isotopic composition of samples
are given in subentries
The gamma ray spectra of the activated targets
were measured using a one of HPGe detectors with
relative efficiencies of 130% resp. 35%. Energy and
efficiency calibrations were performed periodically
throughout the experiment using standard sources.
True-coincidence summing corrections were determined
using a GEANT4 simulation.
Samples were irradiated with proton beam with
energies 8-19 MeV.Beam was collimated and focused to a
circular spot <2mm. Typical proton currents were 400
-700 nA with bombardment times ranging from 5 min to
36h. The beam intensity on target as a function of
time was measured by Faraday cup located 35 cm
downstream of the target. The energy of the beam at
the center of the target was calculated using the
SRIM energy loss tables.
Corrections for high count rates and for possible
interference effects caused by multiple decay
chains were applied
Activities of different reaction products were
determined by analysis of decay rates, which were
followed for several half-lives of each product
nucleus.
Uncertainty from decay curve fitting
Systematic uncertainty from detector
efficiency
Systematic uncertainty of summing
corrections
Systematic uncertainty in nuclear data
Systematic uncertainty in target thickness
Systematic uncertainty in target
composition

correction

determination
